news.headline: Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Dogecoin drop significantly in market turmoil; Bitcoin falls below $100,000. Trump's DJT stock falls by 5%. Musk faces challenges with government ambitions. US stocks face correction amid volatility. Bitcoin liquidations top $1 billion. Musk's involvement in DOGE questioned.
news.summary: Cryptocurrency markets experienced a significant decline with Bitcoin dropping by 10%, Ethereum and XRP by 18%, and Dogecoin by 28%. This drastic change in value has raised concerns about the future outlook of Bitcoin, with some like Gordon Johnson likening it to "rat poison" and calling it "worthless" as it dips below $100,000. On another note, El Salvador continued to invest in Bitcoin despite IMF warnings, purchasing $1 million worth of BTC after suggestions to scale back. Additionally, Shytoshi Kusama announced a partnership between Shiba Inu and Chainlink to enhance the Shibarium ecosystem. Meanwhile, Craig Wright, who claimed to be the inventor of Bitcoin, received a prison sentence for not complying with a court order. The overall crypto market saw liquidations surpassing $1 billion, leading to Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Dogecoin further retracting, with predictions of BTC potentially losing the $96k support. In the US markets, stocks faced potential correction due to volatility, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq ending in the red. Micron's sales miss added to investor sentiment decline, placing the greed index in an "extreme fear" zone. Traders prepared for a lower opening as they braced for the "Triple Witching" hour, but some analysts anticipated a year-end recovery despite the current situation. The article also highlighted five stocks drawing investor attention, including Nike, FedEx, Blackberry, Rigetti, and Tesla. Furthermore, the mention of Elon Musk’s role in Dogecoin was teased, with more details available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Global Bluetooth speaker market to reach $36.06 billion by 2029, from $20.98 billion in 2023, growing at 9.45% CAGR. Report includes data on 60 vendors.
news.summary: A report titled "Bluetooth Speaker Market - Focused Insights 2024-2029" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The global Bluetooth speaker market is projected to achieve $36.06 billion by 2029, a significant increase from $20.98 billion in 2023, indicating a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 9.45% during the period of 2023 to 2029. The comprehensive report covers data on 60 vendors participating in the market. With a blend of established companies and newcomers, the Bluetooth speaker market is fiercely competitive.

news.headline: Stablecoins transforming global payments: Panelist at Benzinga conference says transaction volume nearing double that of Visa, vital for cross-border transactions.
news.summary: During a panel discussion at the Benzinga Future of Digital Assets conference, stablecoins were highlighted as a key player in revolutionizing global payments. CEO of VanEck, Jan van Eck, emphasized stablecoins as a catalyst for a new financial paradigm, especially in regions with inefficient banking systems. Van Eck stated that stablecoins are reshaping the global payments landscape, serving as a foundational element for transactions across borders and enhancing financial inclusivity. In particular, he noted the swift adoption of stablecoins in emerging markets for activities like foreign exchange and remittances. The transaction volume of stablecoins in these markets is now nearing double that of Visa, underscoring their pivotal role in international trade. Despite their expanding usage globally, van Eck pointed out that stablecoins are not fully appreciated in the United States, where traditional banking systems still dominate.

news.headline: Dogecoin price plunges by 21% to 28 cents, alarms traders. Market cap at $42.2 billion. Pepe down 19.7%. Shiba Inu down 17.9%.
news.summary: The sharp decline of Dogecoin (DOGE) by 21% has sparked concern among traders and investors as the cryptocurrency struggles to uphold crucial support levels to prevent further losses. Dogecoin is currently priced at $0.2867 with a market cap of $42.2 billion, showing a 24-hour trend decrease of -21.2% and a 7-day decline of -29.3%. Similarly, Shiba Inu (SHIB) is priced at $0.00001987 with a market cap of $11.7 billion, experiencing a 24-hour decrease of -17.9% and a week-long drop of -29%. Pepe (PEPE) follows suit at $0.00001987 with a market cap of $6.5 billion, showing a 24-hour decrease of -19.7% and a 7-day decline of -34.5%. Notably, crypto trader Kevin correctly predicted Dogecoin's current price range of $0.26–$0.28 on Nov. 13, despite facing criticism. Kevin stands by his viewpoint, emphasizing the developing situation.

news.headline: Detroit Metro Airport to introduce Park Assist solution by TKH Security, aiming to streamline parking for millions of travelers.
news.summary: Enhancing Parking Experience at DTW Airport with TKH Security's Park Assist SolutionCheshire, Connecticut - On December 20, 2024, TKH Security introduced their Park Assist Solution at Detroit Metropolitan Wayne County Airport (DTW) to improve the parking experience for travelers. With the holiday season underway, a record number of passengers are opting for air travel, prompting airports nationwide to enhance their services. By implementing the Park Assist Solution, DTW aims to streamline the parking process and ensure smoother journeys for passengers. This innovation is expected to significantly reduce parking time and enhance overall customer satisfaction at the airport.

The expansion will see a significant increase in production capabilities, with a focus on meeting the growing market requirements. This move reflects the company's commitment to catering to the rising demand for zinc-based energy storage solutions in the United States.

news.headline: Fed's inflation gauge falls, PCE index at 2.4% (Nov 2024), below forecasts; core PCE at 2.8%, incomes rise by 0.3% monthly.
news.summary: In November 2024, the Federal Reserve's key inflation measure, the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index, increased by 2.4% year-over-year, slightly below economist expectations of 2.5%. This was a slight uptick from October's 2.3% and marked the second consecutive monthly rise. On a monthly basis, the PCE index grew by 0.1%, a slowdown from the previous month's 0.2% increase. Excluding volatile elements like food and energy, the core PCE remained unchanged at 2.8% year-over-year, falling short of the anticipated 2.9%. Monthly core PCE growth also decelerated to 0.1% from the prior month's 0.3%, missing expectations of 0.2%. Additionally, personal income saw a 0.3% increase month-over-month in November. This data came as a relief to markets, especially after the Federal Reserve's recent warnings about rising price pressures approaching the new year.

CA RISE allocates second round of funding to 61 social enterprises in California to enhance job opportunities.

news.headline: Global sustainable data center market expected to reach $85.44 billion by 2029, growing at CAGR of 13.71% from 2023 to 2029. Leading tech giants such as Google, Microsoft, Oracle, IBM, Tencent, Meta, and AWS are driving the industry.
news.summary: Dublin - A report titled "Sustainable Data Center Market - Global Outlook & Forecast 2024-2029" has recently been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The global sustainable data center market is projected to see substantial growth, with investments reaching $85.44 billion by 2029, a significant increase from $39.51 billion recorded in 2023. This growth represents a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 13.71% from 2023 to 2029. Key industry players in the data center sector such as Vantage Data Centers, Kao Data, Khazna, Africa Data Centres, STT GDC, GDS, and Equinix are noted for their adoption of HVO fuel.

news.summary: The Personal Consumption Expenditures index rose by 2.4% compared to the previous year. However, the specifics of the report were less optimistic than anticipated.

news.headline: Researchers at Chungnam National University in South Korea have developed a rapid water quality monitoring chip to detect antibiotics amid antimicrobial resistance. Dec 20, 2024. AMR is a critical issue due to bacteria developing resistance to antibiotics.
news.summary: In Daejeon, South Korea, as of December 20, 2024, Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) has emerged as a significant global health concern due to microbes like bacteria developing resistance to antibiotics. The main contributing factor to this escalation is attributed to the improper utilization and disposal of antibiotics in the environment. This trend underscores the urgency of addressing the issue to prevent further proliferation of resistant strains.

news.headline: Brenmiller Energy achieves ISO/IEC 27001 certification for information security, demonstrating commitment to industry standards.
news.summary: Tel Aviv, Israel-based company Brenmiller Energy Ltd. (Nasdaq: BNRG) has recently obtained the ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification, which is recognized as the foremost global standard for Information Security Management Systems (ISMS). As a distinguished provider of thermal energy storage solutions to industrial and utility clients globally, this certification solidifies Brenmiller Energy's commitment to information security. ISO/IEC 27001:2022 is issued by the International Organization for Standardization (ISO), the world's primary independent standards developer.

news.headline: American Rare Earths Limited's subsidiary Wyoming Rare (USA) Inc secures facility in Laramie, Wyoming to support Halleck Creek Project.
news.summary: On December 20, 2024, American Rare Earths Limited (ARR) shared that its subsidiary, Wyoming Rare (USA) Inc., has successfully obtained a facility at the Western Research Institute in Laramie, Wyoming. This advancement signals a crucial milestone in the company's plan to advance the Halleck Creek Rare Earths Project and bolster its operational capacities in the area. ARR is listed as ASX: ARR | OTCQX: ARRNF, AMRRY, and this recent development showcases their commitment to growth and progress.
